title: Harden graceful pod termination in Keystone rolling updates
description:
# GitHub Issue #247: Harden graceful pod termination during Keystone rolling updates

**Category**: improvement | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: `buildKeystoneDeployment` (operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go:138-186) relies on a hardcoded `terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30` paired with a 5 s `preStop` sleep (`/bin/sh -c "sleep 5"`) and uWSGI's `--master` signal handling to drain in-flight requests during rolling updates (e.g. `kubectl patch keystone ... spec.image.tag`). This baseline works for the common case but leaves four production gaps: (1) the 30 s grace period is not user-configurable, leaving only ~25 s for uWSGI to finish in-flight requests — insufficient for workloads with slow LDAP/DB token validation; (2) `uwsgiCommand` (reconcile_deployment.go:278-306) does not set `--harakiri`, so a single hung request can block graceful shutdown until SIGKILL; (3) `--http-keepalive` defaults to `true` (reconcile_deployment.go:281-295) with no keep-alive timeout, letting clients reuse a connection to a pod that has already been removed from the Service endpoints; (4) `Deployment.Spec.Strategy` is never set, so the Kubernetes default `RollingUpdate` with `maxUnavailable: 25%` / `maxSurge: 25%` applies — combined with the `minAvailable: 1` PDB (reconcile_deployment.go:248-271) this permits brief capacity dips rather than zero-downtime rollouts. Expose `spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds`, `spec.preStopSleepSeconds`, `spec.uwsgi.harakiri`, and `spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout` as optional CRD fields; set an explicit `RollingUpdate` strategy with `maxUnavailable: 0`, `maxSurge: 1` as the new default and make it overridable via `spec.strategy`.

**Motivation**: A `kubectl patch keystone ... spec.image.tag` triggers a pod rotation that must deliver both (a) no new requests routed to a terminating pod and (b) in-flight requests completing cleanly. The current implementation mitigates (a) via the 5 s preStop sleep that covers EndpointSlice propagation, but keep-alive connections bypass this protection because clients keep reusing an already-stale socket. (b) is limited by a fixed 25 s drain window without per-worker hang detection — a single stuck token validation blocks shutdown until SIGKILL truncates every other in-flight request on the same pod. Operators cannot tune any of these values today without forking the operator. At the same time, the default `maxUnavailable: 25%` reduces Keystone capacity during rollout — Keystone is the authentication gate for the entire control plane, so any capacity dip amplifies user-visible latency across all OpenStack services.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go`
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go`
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment_test.go`
- `operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml`
- `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml`
- `architecture/docs/04-architecture/04-high-availability.md`

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `KeystoneSpec` gains optional `terminationGracePeriodSeconds *int64` (default 30, min 10) and `preStopSleepSeconds *int64` (default 5, min 0)
- [ ] `UWSGISpec` gains optional `harakiri *int32` (worker request timeout in seconds, default unset) and `httpKeepAliveTimeout *int32` (idle keep-alive timeout in seconds, default unset)
- [ ] `buildKeystoneDeployment` sets `.Spec.Strategy` to `RollingUpdate` with `maxUnavailable: 0` and `maxSurge: 1` by default; new optional `spec.strategy *appsv1.DeploymentStrategy` field allows full override
- [ ] `preStop` sleep duration is derived from `spec.preStopSleepSeconds` instead of the hardcoded `sleep 5`
- [ ] `uwsgiCommand` appends `--harakiri <n>` when set and `--http-keepalive-timeout <n>` when set
- [ ] Validating webhook rejects `preStopSleepSeconds >= terminationGracePeriodSeconds` and rejects `harakiri >= (terminationGracePeriodSeconds - preStopSleepSeconds)` so a worker timeout cannot exceed the remaining drain window
- [ ] Unit tests in `reconcile_deployment_test.go` cover: defaults (grace=30, preStop=5, maxUnavailable=0, maxSurge=1, no harakiri, no keep-alive timeout), all four fields overridden, strategy override replaces defaults, webhook validation paths (preStop >= grace rejected, harakiri too large rejected)
- [ ] E2E test verifies a rolling image-tag patch produces zero failed requests under a steady load generator running against the Service
- [ ] CRD manifests in both `helm/.../crds/` and `config/crd/bases/` are regenerated and committed
- [ ] `architecture/docs/04-architecture/04-high-availability.md` documents the rollout flow (EndpointSlice removal ‖ preStop sleep → SIGTERM → uWSGI graceful shutdown with harakiri fallback → SIGKILL) and the new tunables, including recommended production values and the keep-alive-timeout interaction with the preStop sleep

**Labels:** enhancement

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/247

stories.title: Operator tunes termination grace period for slow LDAP/DB token validation
stories.want: to set spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds on a Keystone CR so I can extend the drain window beyond the hardcoded 30 s
stories.so_that: Keystone pods with slow upstream token validation finish in-flight requests before SIGKILL during rolling updates (CC-0084)
criteria: Setting spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds=120 on a Keystone CR produces a Deployment pod template with TerminationGracePeriodSeconds=120
Omitting spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds yields the default 30 in the generated Deployment
kubectl apply with spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds=5 is rejected by the validating webhook with a 'minimum 10' error
Existing Keystone CRs without the field continue to work unchanged after operator upgrade

stories.title: Operator tunes preStop sleep to cover slow EndpointSlice propagation
stories.want: to set spec.preStopSleepSeconds independently of the grace period
stories.so_that: clusters with slow kube-proxy/EndpointSlice propagation get a longer pre-drain window without extending the overall grace period (CC-0084)
criteria: Setting spec.preStopSleepSeconds=15 produces preStop exec command ['/bin/sh','-c','sleep 15']
Omitting spec.preStopSleepSeconds yields 'sleep 5' in the generated Deployment
spec.preStopSleepSeconds=0 is accepted and produces 'sleep 0'
spec.preStopSleepSeconds=30 with spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds=30 is rejected by the webhook with a message citing the drain-window rule

stories.title: Operator enables uWSGI harakiri to bound per-request shutdown time
stories.want: to set spec.uwsgi.harakiri so a hung request cannot block graceful shutdown beyond a known bound
stories.so_that: a single stuck LDAP lookup does not prevent other in-flight requests on the same pod from completing cleanly (CC-0084)
criteria: Setting spec.uwsgi.harakiri=20 appends '--harakiri 20' to the uWSGI command
Omitting spec.uwsgi.harakiri (nil) keeps the uWSGI command free of --harakiri — no empty flag is emitted
spec.uwsgi.harakiri=1 is accepted (minimum is 1)
spec.uwsgi.harakiri=60 with spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds=30 and spec.preStopSleepSeconds=5 is rejected by the webhook because harakiri >= (grace - preStop)

stories.title: Operator configures HTTP keep-alive timeout to force reconnection after pod removal
stories.want: to set spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout so idle keep-alive connections expire promptly
stories.so_that: clients reconnect via the Service and are routed to healthy pods instead of reusing a socket to a terminating pod (CC-0084)
criteria: Setting spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout=5 appends '--http-keepalive-timeout 5' to the uWSGI command
Omitting spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout (nil) keeps the uWSGI command free of the flag
spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout=0 is rejected by the webhook (minimum 1) to prevent an unbounded-timeout interpretation
spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout=3 combined with spec.preStopSleepSeconds=5 is accepted (recommended: keep-alive-timeout <= preStop sleep)

stories.title: Operator gets zero-downtime rolling updates out of the box
stories.want: buildKeystoneDeployment to set an explicit RollingUpdate strategy with maxUnavailable=0 and maxSurge=1 by default
stories.so_that: image tag patches to a multi-replica Keystone never dip available capacity below the configured replica count (CC-0084)
criteria: A Deployment built for a Keystone CR without spec.strategy carries Spec.Strategy.Type=RollingUpdate with RollingUpdate.MaxUnavailable=0 and MaxSurge=1
Setting spec.strategy with a full DeploymentStrategy object replaces the defaults verbatim
spec.strategy with Type=Recreate is propagated unchanged (no injection of RollingUpdate block)
Omitting spec.strategy on an existing CR does not trigger an unnecessary Deployment update if the existing Strategy already matches the new defaults

stories.title: Operator can fully override the rollout strategy when site policy requires it
stories.want: to set spec.strategy to an arbitrary valid appsv1.DeploymentStrategy
stories.so_that: I can switch to Recreate or customize maxSurge/maxUnavailable percentages for specific clusters without forking the operator (CC-0084)
criteria: spec.strategy with Type=RollingUpdate, maxUnavailable=50%, maxSurge=0 round-trips through the reconciler unchanged
spec.strategy with Type=Recreate produces Deployment.Spec.Strategy.Type=Recreate and omits the RollingUpdate block
Invalid spec.strategy (e.g., both Type=Recreate and RollingUpdate block set) is rejected by the webhook
CRD OpenAPI schema accepts the full appsv1.DeploymentStrategy shape via kubebuilder generation

stories.title: SRE verifies zero failed requests during image-tag rollout
stories.want: an end-to-end test that runs a steady HTTP load against the Keystone Service during an image-tag rolling update
stories.so_that: regressions in the graceful-termination chain (strategy, preStop, harakiri, keep-alive) are caught in CI (CC-0084)
criteria: E2E test applies a Keystone CR with replicas>=3 and waits for Ready=True
A load generator pod sends at least 200 requests over 60 s to the Service during a kubectl patch that bumps spec.image.tag
The test asserts the load generator recorded zero 5xx or connection-reset responses
The test asserts Deployment.status.availableReplicas never drops below Spec.Replicas during the rollout
Test skipped or guarded in sandbox-only CI lanes that lack a live cluster; runs in e2e chainsaw lane

stories.title: Operator reads authoritative tunable documentation
stories.want: architecture/docs/04-architecture/04-high-availability.md to document the rollout flow and the new tunables
stories.so_that: I can pick recommended values for terminationGracePeriodSeconds, preStopSleepSeconds, harakiri, and httpKeepAliveTimeout without reading operator source (CC-0084)
criteria: The HA doc contains a 'Keystone rolling update' subsection with a sequence diagram: EndpointSlice removal ‖ preStop sleep → SIGTERM → uWSGI graceful shutdown with harakiri fallback → SIGKILL
The doc lists recommended production values for each new field and explains the keep-alive-timeout ≤ preStopSleepSeconds interaction
The doc explains the new default RollingUpdate strategy (maxUnavailable=0, maxSurge=1) and when operators should override it
Every feature reference uses the CC-0084 identifier

stories.title: Developer updates the keystone-crd reference after schema change
stories.want: docs/reference/keystone-crd.md to describe the new KeystoneSpec and UWSGISpec fields
stories.so_that: the generated reference documentation stays consistent with the CRD schema (CC-0084)
criteria: docs/reference/keystone-crd.md describes spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds, spec.preStopSleepSeconds, and spec.strategy with defaults and min values
docs/reference/keystone-crd.md describes spec.uwsgi.harakiri and spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout with defaults (unset) and min values
Reference doc cross-links to the HA doc rollout-flow section
Every new field entry carries the CC-0084 identifier

requirements.description: The KeystoneSpec SHALL expose terminationGracePeriodSeconds as an optional *int64 field with default 30 and minimum 10
requirements.rationale: Operators need to tune the drain window for slow upstream token validation without forking the operator (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Unset yields default 30
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created without spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds
scenarios.then: the generated Deployment pod template carries TerminationGracePeriodSeconds=30
and_then: webhook Default() leaves the pointer nil so an upgrade does not mutate existing CRs

scenarios.name: Custom value propagated
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created with spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds=120
scenarios.then: the generated Deployment pod template carries TerminationGracePeriodSeconds=120

scenarios.name: Below minimum rejected
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created with spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds=5
scenarios.then: the validating webhook returns an Invalid error citing 'minimum 10'
and_then: no Deployment is created or updated

requirements.description: The KeystoneSpec SHALL expose preStopSleepSeconds as an optional *int64 field with default 5 and minimum 0
requirements.rationale: Operators need to adjust the pre-drain window independently of the overall grace period (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Unset yields default sleep 5
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created without spec.preStopSleepSeconds
scenarios.then: the preStop exec command is ['/bin/sh','-c','sleep 5']

scenarios.name: Custom value propagated
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created with spec.preStopSleepSeconds=15
scenarios.then: the preStop exec command is ['/bin/sh','-c','sleep 15']

scenarios.name: Zero is valid
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR is created with spec.preStopSleepSeconds=0
scenarios.then: the preStop exec command is ['/bin/sh','-c','sleep 0'] and the webhook accepts it
and_then: the command is still emitted so the pod template shape is stable

requirements.description: The UWSGISpec SHALL expose harakiri as an optional *int32 field (no default) with minimum 1
requirements.rationale: A per-request hang cap prevents a single stuck request from blocking graceful shutdown until SIGKILL (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Unset omits the flag
scenarios.when: spec.uwsgi.harakiri is nil (or spec.uwsgi is nil)
scenarios.then: uwsgiCommand does not emit --harakiri

scenarios.name: Set emits the flag
scenarios.then: uwsgiCommand includes '--harakiri','20' in that order

scenarios.name: Below minimum rejected
scenarios.then: the validating webhook returns an Invalid error citing 'minimum 1'

requirements.description: The UWSGISpec SHALL expose httpKeepAliveTimeout as an optional *int32 field (no default) with minimum 1
requirements.rationale: Bounded idle keep-alive forces clients to reconnect through the Service so they never reuse a socket to a removed pod (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Unset omits the flag
scenarios.then: uwsgiCommand does not emit --http-keepalive-timeout

scenarios.name: Set emits the flag
scenarios.then: uwsgiCommand includes '--http-keepalive-timeout','5'
and_then: flag is appended only when httpKeepAlive is also true, since the timeout is meaningless without keep-alive

scenarios.name: Below minimum rejected
scenarios.then: the validating webhook returns an Invalid error citing 'minimum 1'

requirements.description: buildKeystoneDeployment SHALL set Spec.Strategy to RollingUpdate with maxUnavailable=0 and maxSurge=1 by default
requirements.rationale: Prevents brief capacity dips during rollout by requiring a surge pod to become Ready before any pod is removed (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Default strategy
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has no spec.strategy
scenarios.then: the generated Deployment Spec.Strategy.Type equals RollingUpdate and RollingUpdate.MaxUnavailable=IntOrString{Type:Int,IntVal:0} and MaxSurge=IntOrString{Type:Int,IntVal:1}

scenarios.name: Strategy stable across reconciles
scenarios.when: the reconciler runs twice against a CR without spec.strategy
scenarios.then: the second call produces the same Deployment strategy object
and_then: EnsureDeployment does not treat defaults as drift

scenarios.name: Works with replicas=1
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR has replicas=1 and no spec.strategy
scenarios.then: the Deployment still uses maxUnavailable=0 and maxSurge=1 — the PDB path already handles single-replica eviction separately

requirements.description: The KeystoneSpec SHALL expose strategy as an optional *appsv1.DeploymentStrategy field that fully overrides the defaults
requirements.rationale: Operators with site-specific constraints need to switch to Recreate or non-zero maxUnavailable without operator changes (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Custom RollingUpdate propagated
scenarios.when: spec.strategy is set to RollingUpdate with maxUnavailable=50% and maxSurge=0
scenarios.then: the Deployment Spec.Strategy reflects those exact values and the defaults are NOT merged in

scenarios.name: Recreate propagated
scenarios.then: the Deployment Spec.Strategy.Type=Recreate and Spec.Strategy.RollingUpdate is nil

scenarios.name: Invalid combination rejected
scenarios.when: spec.strategy.Type=Recreate is set together with a non-nil RollingUpdate block
scenarios.then: the validating webhook returns an Invalid error

requirements.description: The validating webhook SHALL reject preStopSleepSeconds >= terminationGracePeriodSeconds
requirements.rationale: A preStop sleep that equals or exceeds the grace period leaves zero time for uWSGI to drain (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Equal rejected
scenarios.then: the webhook returns Invalid with field path spec.preStopSleepSeconds citing the drain-window rule

scenarios.name: Greater rejected
scenarios.then: the webhook returns Invalid

scenarios.name: Strictly less accepted
scenarios.then: validation passes

requirements.description: The validating webhook SHALL reject spec.uwsgi.harakiri >= (terminationGracePeriodSeconds - preStopSleepSeconds)
requirements.rationale: A harakiri timeout longer than the post-preStop drain window gives no safety margin before SIGKILL (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: At boundary rejected
scenarios.then: the webhook returns Invalid citing 'harakiri must be < (terminationGracePeriodSeconds - preStopSleepSeconds)'

scenarios.name: Above boundary rejected
scenarios.then: the webhook returns Invalid

scenarios.name: Strictly less accepted
scenarios.then: validation passes
and_then: validation uses resolved defaults: unset grace=30, unset preStop=5

requirements.description: buildKeystoneDeployment SHALL derive the preStop exec command from the effective preStopSleepSeconds value
requirements.rationale: The hardcoded 'sleep 5' must be replaced so the tunable takes effect at rollout time (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Effective default
scenarios.then: preStop command is ['/bin/sh','-c','sleep 5']

scenarios.name: Effective custom
scenarios.then: preStop command is ['/bin/sh','-c','sleep 15']

scenarios.name: Effective zero
scenarios.then: preStop command is ['/bin/sh','-c','sleep 0'] — the Lifecycle block is still emitted to keep the pod template shape stable

requirements.description: buildKeystoneDeployment SHALL pass the effective terminationGracePeriodSeconds to the PodSpec
requirements.rationale: Replaces the hardcoded 30 so the new spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds field actually takes effect (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Default applied
scenarios.then: PodSpec.TerminationGracePeriodSeconds=ptr.To(int64(30))

scenarios.name: Custom applied
scenarios.then: PodSpec.TerminationGracePeriodSeconds=ptr.To(int64(120))

scenarios.name: Kubebuilder minimum enforced at schema layer
scenarios.then: the CRD OpenAPI rejects the value with minimum=10 before reaching the webhook

requirements.description: uwsgiCommand SHALL append --harakiri <n> and --http-keepalive-timeout <n> in a deterministic order after existing flags
requirements.rationale: Deterministic ordering keeps the generated PodSpec hash-stable so EnsureDeployment does not detect false drift (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Both set
scenarios.then: uwsgiCommand contains '--harakiri','20','--http-keepalive-timeout','5' in that order appended after existing flags

scenarios.name: Only harakiri set
scenarios.then: uwsgiCommand contains '--harakiri','20' but not '--http-keepalive-timeout'

scenarios.name: httpKeepAlive=false with timeout set
scenarios.then: uwsgiCommand omits both '--http-keepalive' and '--http-keepalive-timeout' since the timeout is meaningless without keep-alive
and_then: the webhook warns or rejects this combination — see REQ-012

requirements.description: The validating webhook SHALL reject spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout when spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAlive is explicitly false
requirements.rationale: A keep-alive timeout on a connection mode that disables keep-alive is a user configuration error we catch early (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Disabled with timeout rejected
scenarios.then: the webhook returns Invalid at spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout citing 'requires httpKeepAlive=true'

scenarios.name: Enabled with timeout accepted
scenarios.then: validation passes

scenarios.name: Disabled without timeout accepted
scenarios.then: validation passes

requirements.description: CRD manifests in both helm and config/crd/bases SHALL be regenerated so the OpenAPI schema matches the Go types
requirements.rationale: Without regeneration, API clients and kubectl explain present stale field information (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Helm CRD contains new fields
scenarios.then: operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml contains terminationGracePeriodSeconds, preStopSleepSeconds, strategy, uwsgi.harakiri, uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout

scenarios.name: Config CRD contains new fields
scenarios.then: operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml contains the same fields
and_then: both files stay byte-identical for the shared schema block

scenarios.name: Minimum markers present
scenarios.then: terminationGracePeriodSeconds minimum=10, preStopSleepSeconds minimum=0, harakiri minimum=1, httpKeepAliveTimeout minimum=1 are emitted

requirements.description: The E2E chainsaw test SHALL verify zero 5xx/connection-reset responses during a rolling image-tag patch
requirements.rationale: Asserts the end-to-end graceful-termination chain (strategy + preStop + keep-alive + harakiri) against a real cluster (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Happy-path rollout
scenarios.when: a load generator issues ≥200 GET /v3 requests over 60 s while spec.image.tag is patched
scenarios.then: the generator reports 0 responses with status >=500 and 0 connection resets

scenarios.name: Rollout completes
scenarios.then: status.conditions[Ready]=True within 5 minutes

scenarios.name: Sandbox safety
scenarios.when: the test is invoked without a live kind/chainsaw cluster
scenarios.then: it is skipped (chainsaw lane only) rather than failing with non-actionable errors

requirements.description: The high-availability architecture doc SHALL document the Keystone rollout flow and the new tunables
requirements.rationale: Operators need authoritative guidance on picking production values and on the keep-alive/preStop interaction (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Rollout flow described
scenarios.then: a 'Keystone rolling update' section explains the sequence EndpointSlice removal ‖ preStop sleep → SIGTERM → uWSGI graceful shutdown with harakiri fallback → SIGKILL

scenarios.name: Recommended values table
scenarios.then: a table lists recommended production values for each new field with rationale

scenarios.name: Default strategy explained
scenarios.then: the new RollingUpdate default (maxUnavailable=0, maxSurge=1) is documented alongside guidance on when to override it

requirements.description: The keystone-crd reference doc SHALL describe every new field with type, default, minimum, and effect
requirements.rationale: Keeps the CRD reference doc consistent with the new schema so CR authors do not have to read Go source (CC-0084)

scenarios.name: Fields listed
scenarios.then: spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds, spec.preStopSleepSeconds, spec.strategy, spec.uwsgi.harakiri, spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout are documented

scenarios.name: Cross-links present
scenarios.then: each entry links to the HA doc rollout-flow section for rationale

scenarios.name: CC identifiers present
scenarios.then: every new entry carries the CC-0084 identifier

tasks.title: Add terminationGracePeriodSeconds and preStopSleepSeconds *int64 fields to KeystoneSpec in operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go with kubebuilder markers (Minimum=10/default=30 and Minimum=0/default=5). Update zz_generated.deepcopy.go via controller-gen. Unit test TestKeystoneSpecTerminationGracePeriodSecondsField in keystone_types_test.go (REQ-001, REQ-002). Feature: CC-0084.

Add harakiri and httpKeepAliveTimeout *int32 fields to UWSGISpec in keystone_types.go with kubebuilder Minimum=1 markers (no defaults). Update zz_generated.deepcopy.go. Extend TestUWSGISpecFields to cover the new fields (REQ-003, REQ-004). Feature: CC-0084.

Add strategy *appsv1.DeploymentStrategy optional field to KeystoneSpec in keystone_types.go with kubebuilder marker allowing full shape. Update imports (k8s.io/api/apps/v1). Regenerate deepcopy. Minimal unit test asserting field is settable (REQ-006). Feature: CC-0084.

Add webhook range checks for terminationGracePeriodSeconds (>=10) and preStopSleepSeconds (>=0) in operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go validate(). Add tests TestValidate_TerminationGracePeriodBelowMinRejected and companions in keystone_webhook_test.go (REQ-001, REQ-002). Feature: CC-0084.

Add webhook cross-field check rejecting preStopSleepSeconds >= terminationGracePeriodSeconds (resolving nil to defaults 5/30) in validate(). Tests TestValidate_PreStop{Equals,Exceeds,StrictlyLess}* in keystone_webhook_test.go (REQ-007). Feature: CC-0084.

Add webhook range checks for spec.uwsgi.harakiri (>=1) and spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout (>=1) in validate(). Tests TestValidate_UWSGIHarakiriBelowMinRejected and TestValidate_UWSGIKeepAliveTimeoutBelowMinRejected (REQ-003, REQ-004). Feature: CC-0084.

Add webhook cross-field check rejecting harakiri >= (terminationGracePeriodSeconds - preStopSleepSeconds) using resolved defaults. Tests TestValidate_HarakiriAtDrainBoundaryRejected/AboveDrainRejected/WithinDrainAccepted (REQ-008). Feature: CC-0084.

Add webhook cross-field check rejecting httpKeepAliveTimeout set when httpKeepAlive=false. Tests TestValidate_KeepAliveTimeoutWithoutKeepAliveRejected and companion accepted case (REQ-012). Feature: CC-0084.

Add webhook validation rejecting spec.strategy with Type=Recreate combined with non-nil RollingUpdate block. Test TestValidate_StrategyRecreateWithRollingUpdateBlockRejected (REQ-006). Feature: CC-0084.

Update buildKeystoneDeployment in operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_deployment.go to derive PodSpec.TerminationGracePeriodSeconds from spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds (fallback 30) and the preStop exec command from spec.preStopSleepSeconds (fallback 5, emit 'sleep <n>' via fmt.Sprintf). Tests TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_TerminationGracePeriod* and PreStopSleep* in reconcile_deployment_test.go (REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-009, REQ-010). Feature: CC-0084.

Update uwsgiCommand in reconcile_deployment.go to append '--harakiri <n>' when UWSGISpec.Harakiri is non-nil and '--http-keepalive-timeout <n>' when UWSGISpec.HTTPKeepAliveTimeout is non-nil AND httpKeepAlive is true. Update the doc comment. Tests TestUwsgiCommand_{Harakiri,KeepAliveTimeout}* and TestUwsgiCommand_FlagOrderDeterministic plus KeepAliveTimeoutIgnoredWhenKeepAliveDisabled (REQ-003, REQ-004, REQ-011). Feature: CC-0084.

Update buildKeystoneDeployment to set Deployment.Spec.Strategy: use spec.strategy verbatim when non-nil, otherwise inject RollingUpdate with MaxUnavailable=IntOrString(0) and MaxSurge=IntOrString(1). Extract helper deploymentStrategy(keystone) for clarity. Tests TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_DefaultRollingUpdateStrategy, StrategyStableAcrossReconciles, StrategyOverrideRollingCustomPercents, StrategyOverrideRecreate (REQ-005, REQ-006). Feature: CC-0084.

Verify EnsureDeployment in internal/common/deployment does not report drift when spec.Strategy is defaulted on a pre-existing Deployment that lacked one. If drift logic needs adjustment, update it; otherwise add a test note and a TestBuildKeystoneDeployment_StrategyStableAcrossReconciles assertion (REQ-005). Feature: CC-0084.

Add envtest integration tests TestIntegration_TerminationGracePeriodAppliedToDeployment, TestIntegration_DefaultStrategyAppliedToDeployment, TestIntegration_StrategyOverrideAppliedToDeployment in operators/keystone/internal/controller/integration_test.go (REQ-001, REQ-005, REQ-006). Feature: CC-0084.

Regenerate CRD manifests via 'make manifests' (or equivalent target). Commit operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/crds/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml and operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml. Verify new fields and minimums appear (REQ-013). Feature: CC-0084.

Create tests/e2e/keystone/rolling-update-zero-downtime/ with 00-keystone-cr.yaml (replicas=3, spec.strategy defaults), 01-patch-image.yaml, and chainsaw-test.yaml. The test: (a) waits for Ready, (b) runs a load-generator Job (curl in a shell loop or vegeta sidecar) at steady rate for 60 s while kubectl patch bumps image tag, (c) asserts zero 5xx/connection-reset from the load generator logs, (d) asserts Deployment.status.availableReplicas stays >= spec.replicas. Keep the test under the chainsaw e2e lane (REQ-014). Feature: CC-0084.

Update architecture/docs/04-architecture/04-high-availability.md: add a 'Keystone rolling update' subsection with an ASCII sequence diagram (EndpointSlice removal ‖ preStop sleep → SIGTERM → uWSGI graceful shutdown with harakiri fallback → SIGKILL), a tunables table with recommended production values, and the httpKeepAliveTimeout ≤ preStopSleepSeconds interaction note. Reference CC-0084 on every addition (REQ-015). Feature: CC-0084.

Update docs/reference/keystone-crd.md with entries for spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds, spec.preStopSleepSeconds, spec.strategy, spec.uwsgi.harakiri, spec.uwsgi.httpKeepAliveTimeout (type, default, minimum, effect). Cross-link to the HA rollout-flow section. Reference CC-0084 on every new entry (REQ-016). Feature: CC-0084.

review_criteria: BLOCKER: All SHALL requirements REQ-001..REQ-016 have passing unit, integration, and (where applicable) E2E tests
BLOCKER: buildKeystoneDeployment always sets Spec.Strategy — no path leaves it at zero value
BLOCKER: Webhook cross-field validation (REQ-007, REQ-008, REQ-012, REQ-006) correctly resolves nil terminationGracePeriodSeconds/preStopSleepSeconds to defaults (30/5) before evaluating the rule
BLOCKER: uwsgiCommand never emits a flag with an empty value; nil pointers are strictly gated
CRITICAL: CRD OpenAPI schema in both helm/ and config/crd/bases/ is byte-identical for the shared block and contains the new fields with correct minimums

CRITICAL: Existing Keystone CRs without any new field continue to produce Deployments equivalent to the old behavior (grace=30, preStop sleep 5, no harakiri, no keep-alive-timeout) — backward compatibility verified by an explicit test
CRITICAL: E2E test passes under the chainsaw lane with zero 5xx/connection-reset across ≥200 requests during the image-tag patch
CRITICAL: Code and comments consistently cite CC-0084 (per Feature ID rule)
MINOR: Go code passes go vet and golangci-lint; no type: ignore-equivalents
MINOR: No unrelated refactors in reconcile_deployment.go beyond the helper extraction for deploymentStrategy() and the preStop/grace plumbing
MINOR: Documentation updates use the existing heading depth and table style of the surrounding files
implementation_notes:
Architectural decisions:

1) Pointer types (*int64/*int32) for all new optional fields so the webhook can distinguish 'unset' from the zero value. terminationGracePeriodSeconds and preStopSleepSeconds are *int64 to match corev1.PodSpec.TerminationGracePeriodSeconds. harakiri and httpKeepAliveTimeout are *int32 to match existing UWSGISpec integers.

2) Defaults live in buildKeystoneDeployment (fallback when nil), not in the defaulting webhook. Reason: keeping defaults out of the stored object means operators who later want 'just use the current default' do not have to chase drift when the operator changes defaults. This matches the existing uwsgi nil-vs-zero pattern (see keystone_webhook.go:93-100 comment on HTTPKeepAlive).

3) Kubebuilder markers set the schema minimum/default. Webhook performs defense-in-depth checks identical to the existing UWSGI/Resources pattern (keystone_webhook.go:276-292).

4) Strategy override is stored verbatim — no merging. When spec.strategy is non-nil, use it exactly; otherwise emit the new default. This mirrors how TopologySpreadConstraints handles user-provided-vs-default (reconcile_deployment.go:324-343).

5) Flag ordering in uwsgiCommand is deterministic: existing flags stay in their current positions; new flags (--harakiri, --http-keepalive-timeout) are appended at the end, in that order. Keeps PodSpec hash stable across reconciles.

6) REQ-011 edge case: if httpKeepAlive=false, neither --http-keepalive nor --http-keepalive-timeout is emitted. The webhook (REQ-012) rejects the combination at admission so the runtime code path is never reached with an invalid CR, but the command-builder still guards against it defensively.

7) CRD regeneration is required because we add fields to two files (helm/ and config/crd/bases/). The project's Makefile has a 'manifests' target via controller-gen; run it locally and commit the diff rather than hand-editing yaml.

Data flow (CC-0084):
  Keystone CR → Default webhook (no new defaults set) → Validate webhook (range + cross-field checks REQ-001..REQ-008, REQ-012) → Reconciler → buildKeystoneDeployment → Deployment
    ├── PodSpec.TerminationGracePeriodSeconds ← spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds or 30
    ├── Container.Lifecycle.PreStop.Exec ← ['/bin/sh','-c','sleep '+spec.preStopSleepSeconds or 5]
    ├── Container.Command ← uwsgiCommand(spec.uwsgi) with --harakiri/--http-keepalive-timeout appended when set
    └── Spec.Strategy ← spec.strategy verbatim OR RollingUpdate{MaxUnavailable:0, MaxSurge:1}

Architecture diagram (ASCII):
  ┌──────────────┐    ┌──────────────────┐    ┌──────────────────────┐
  │ Keystone CR  │──▶ │ Validate webhook │──▶ │ buildKeystoneDeploy- │
  │ (spec)       │    │ (REQ-7/8/12)     │    │ ment (REQ-5/9/10/11) │
  └──────────────┘    └──────────────────┘    └──────────┬───────────┘
                                                         ▼
                                                 appsv1.Deployment
                                                 ├── Spec.Strategy
                                                 ├── PodSpec.TGPS
                                                 ├── Container.Lifecycle.PreStop
                                                 └── Container.Command (uwsgi)

Potential pitfalls:
  - Do NOT set spec.terminationGracePeriodSeconds or preStopSleepSeconds in Default(). Keeping pointer nil on stored CRs preserves backwards compatibility and avoids churn for every existing CR on operator upgrade.
  - Webhook cross-field checks must resolve nil→default BEFORE applying the inequality (REQ-007, REQ-008). Skipping this would make nil values silently bypass the rule.
  - When spec.strategy is provided, DO NOT merge with defaults. Operators who set maxUnavailable=50% expect exactly 50%, not 50% merged with surge=1.
  - preStopSleepSeconds=0 still produces a Lifecycle block so EnsureDeployment doesn't detect spurious drift when toggling back and forth.

Patterns to follow:
  - UWSGI fields: keystone_webhook.go:274-292 (defense-in-depth) and keystone_webhook.go:86-100 (documentation of nil vs zero).
  - Optional pointer fields: TrustFlushSpec and Autoscaling (keystone_types.go:78, 107).
  - Defaulting pointer to literal: ptr.To(int64(30)) pattern already used in reconcile_deployment.go:138.
  - Table-driven tests: see existing reconcile_deployment_test.go style.
  - E2E chainsaw structure: tests/e2e/keystone/graceful-shutdown/ and tests/e2e/keystone/image-upgrade/ provide the template for the new rolling-update-zero-downtime test.
